title: DATABASE CONSTRAINT AND RULE LEARNING USING LARGE LANGUAGE MODELS
summary:
A method for database constraint generation, executed by at least one processor on a computing device accessing one or more large language models (LLMs), comprising retrieving data and/or metadata from a database; generating prompts by parameterizing inputs with concrete values;
 interacting with LLMs through these prompts to obtain and analyze responses; and performing data intelligence processing to derive natural-language descriptions of structural database elements. The method enables generating database constraints from defined classes, such as attribute-domain restrictions, intra-relational, and inter-relational constraints. Constraints include semantic, syntactic, and dependency-based types. Orchestration of constraint learning involves predefined or dynamic workflows incorporating tasks like database sampling, constraint
testing, and refinement. It employs LLM-based techniques to generate candidate rules and optimize constraints through iterative testing and scoring. The method further supports counterexample identification, score aggregation, and rule evaluation to ensure robust constraint
generation and refinement.
181

details:
Amendments to the Claims:
This listing of claims will replace all prior versions, and listings, of claims in the application:
Listing of the Claims:
1
1. (Currently Amended) A method of database constraint generation executed by at least one processor on at least one computing device and accessing one or more large language models (LLMs), the method comprising:
(i) accessing a database and retrieving at least one from data and/or metadata related tefromthe database,
(ii) generating prompts to be answered by the one or more LLMs, wherein at least one prompt is generated from a parameterized prompt by replacing parameters in the parameterized prompt with concrete values,
(iii) interacting with the one or more LLMs by sending the generated prompts to the one or more LLMs, receiving prompt-answers from the one or more LLMs, and analyzing the prompt-answers,
(iv) obtaining intelligence about a nature of data and relationships in the database or in a sub-structure of the database by automatically performing data intelligence information processing for gathering verbal descriptions of structural database elements and providing the verbal descriptions to the one or more LLMs, the data intelligence information processing comprising generating one or more data-intelligence prompts of prompt types selected from a set of prompt types comprising:

(a) prompts containing retrieved data from the database to inform one or more LLMs about the nature of the data and the relationships contained in the database or the database sub-structure,
(b) prompts that use one or more retrieved metadata items from the database to inform one or more LLMs about the nature of the data and the relationships contained in the database, and
(c) prompts that ask for one or more natural-language descriptions of at least one from database elements and/or database schema elements, wherein the at least one from database elements and/or database schema elements, when the database is relational, comprising comprises data relations and attributes, the one or more natural-language descriptions to be used within subsequently generated prompts, and

(v) generating, using one or more parameterized prompts, at least one database constraint, the at least one database constraint belonging to at least one constraint class from a set of constraint classes comprising:(a) attribute-domain restrictions that restrict the possible domain values of attributes of entities, when the database is a relational database the possible domain values of attributes of a data relation, the constraint class of attribute-domain restrictions comprising constraint of constraint types comprising:
semantic (a.k.a. ontological) attribute-domain restrictions that restrict data items or data values to be of a certain semantic category, the category being expressed in natural language, and

syntactic attribute-value constraints, that express restrictions to attribute- values, the restrictions being syntactically verifiable or checkable,
(b) when the database is relational, intra-relational constraints that express data dependencies within a data table or data relation, and
(c) when the database is relational, inter-relational data dependencies that express data dependencies between different data relations.

Currently amended

2
2. (Currently Amended) The method of Claim claim 1,

wherein previous prompt- answers are used to determine one or more prompts to be sent to the one or more LLMs.
Currently amended

3
3. (Currently Amended) The method of Claim claim 1,

wherein at least two different prompts are respectively generated from at least two different parameterized prompts by replacing parameters in the parameterized prompts with concrete values.
Currently amended

4
4. (Currently Amended) The method of Claim claim 1, 
wherein the database is of a type from a set of database types comprising:
(i) Relational databases, which organize and store data in a tabular format with predefined schemas,
(ii) Non-relational databases,
(iii) File-Based Databases or datasets, and
(iv) Vector databases.

Currently amended

5
5. (Currently Amended) The method of Claim claim 1, 
wherein at least one constraint is from a set of constraint types comprising: Datalog rules, Vadalog rules, Prolog rules, rules conforming to a variant or extension of Datalog or Prolog, constraints expressed in first-order logic, tuple-generating dependencies (TGDs), numerical constraints, logical formulas containing numerical predicates, database dependencies, denial constraints and constraints expressed in natural language.
Currently amended

6
6. (Currently Amended) The method of Claim claim 1, 
wherein sending prompts to the one or more LLMs comprises jointly sending two or more of the generated prompts to the one or more LLMs in the form of a single comprehensive prompt.
Currently amended

7
7. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the one or more metadata items from the database are obtained from at least one from:
(i) a data dictionary or a similar component of the database, and/or (ii) other available metadata not being part of a data dictionary, the one or more metadata items comprising textual descriptions.
Currently amended

8
8. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 1, 
wherein at least one from:
(i) a class of the syntactic attribute-value constraints comprises at least one from:(a) range constraints comprising the restriction of numerical or lexicographic values to one or more ranges, and/or

(b) length constraints, restricting the length of text strings or similar data items,
and/or (c) data format constraints, that impose one or more syntactical formats to attribute values, and/or (d) enumerated constraints that require a data item to belong to one or more predefined sets of explicitly enumerated values, and/or (e) regular expression constraints that require a data item to match the pattern defined by a regular expression, and/or (ii) a class of the intra-relational constraints comprises one or more dependencies from a set of one or more types of dependencies comprising:(a) functional dependencies (FDs),
(b) conditional functional dependencies (CFDs),
(c) key constraints,
(d) tuple-generating dependencies (TGDs) that are intra-relational,
(e) multi-valued dependencies, and
(f) join dependencies, and/or (iii) a class of the inter-relational constraints comprises one or more dependencies from a set of one or more types of dependencies comprising:
(a) inter-relational TGDs,
(b) inclusion dependencies (IDs),
and
(c) foreign keys, and other referential integrity constraints.

Currently amended

10
10. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 1, 
further comprising:
(i) associating scores to records of the database, where each of the scores expresses at least one from a degree of certainty and/or a fuzzy truth value, and

(ii) evaluating at least one tuple generating dependency (TGDs) over the database, where one or more body atoms of the at least one TGD match facts that correspond to, or are identified with, database records having associated scores, and
(iii) aggregating the scores such thatby a score aggreg
ation which, in case a TGD is applicable and in case the TGDs rule body matches one or more scored facts, assigns a score is assigned to the resulting atom, that is, to the head atom instance obtained by applying the TGD, the score aggregating aggreg
ation being selected from a set of score aggregation methods comprising:(a) implicit fuzzy score aggregation, wherein a conjunction in a TGD body is interpreted as a fuzzy conjunction according to some T-norm of fuzzy logic, and
(b) explicit score aggregation, wherein the scores of each matched scored fact are contained in a distinguished argument position of the fact, and wherein the aggregation of the scores of the matched scored facts is explicitly computed by the evaluation of arithmetic atoms contained in the TGDs rule body, and
(c) scaled score aggregation, wherein the scores of each matched scored fact are aggregated using at least one arithmetic formula, in which one or more scores or sub- formulas are scaled by coefficients.

Currently amended

11
11. (Original) The method of claim 10, 
wherein the score aggregating comprises scaled score aggregation, and wherein the coefficients that scale the one or more scores or sub- formulas are determined by at least one machine learning method using as dataset the database or a subset of the database.

Original

12
12. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 1, 
further comprising orchestrating constraint learning, the constraint learning orchestration automatically orchestrating a plurality of steps according to one or a combination of:

(a) a predefined fixed workflow,

and
(b) a dynamic workflow wherein an order and number of repetition of steps is dynamically determined at runtime and wherein execution of at least one step depends on answers from the one or more LLMSs to prompts issued in one or more previous steps,
the constraint learning orchestration initiating, monitoring, repeating where necessary, and controlling multiple steps, each step automatically carrying out one task from a set of tasks comprising:(i) generation of sample databases, whereby at least one sample database is obtained from the database, wherein the at least one sample-database comprises selected or sampled records from the database,
(ii) database understanding,
(iii) the generating at least one database constraint, comprising at least one from:(a) non-LLM-based constraint generation, whereby at least one from known statistical methods and/or known methods of constraint or rule learning are applied to the database or to the at least one sample-database to generate high- confidence constraints, and/or (b) generation of attribute-domain restrictions, and/or

(c) when the database is a relational database or a database that can be interpretedis interpretable as a relational database, bag-template-based rule generation with help of LLMs, wherein at least one prompt is issued to the one or more LLMs, the at least one prompt asking for one or more rules, each rule in a form of a tuple-generating dependency (TGD), the TGD required to be of a specified maximum length and the TGD required to conform to at least one specified bag-template, the at least one bag-template imposing restrictions on the set of predicates allowed to occur as part of atoms in the rule-body of the TGD ("allowed predicates"), and, for at least one allowed predicate, the at least one bag-template restricting the number of occurrences of the at least one predicate in the rule body of the TGD, and/or (d) direct generation of classical data dependencies, wherein, for at least one type of classical database dependencies, at least one prompt is issued to the one or more LLMs asking for the recognition and generation of a set of classical dependencies deemed to hold in the database,

(iv) constraint testing, wherein a generated candidate constraint is evaluated over the database or over at least one sample database, the at least one sample database obtained from the database, and wherein strength metrics of the constraint relative to the database or sample database are computed, the computed strength metrics selected from a set of strength metrics, the set comprising confidence and support,
(v) selection of candidate constraints, wherein, according to predefined criteria based on strength-metric
s values, at least one from:

(a) candidate constraints are retained for further processing, and/or (b) candidate constraints are rejected and eliminated, and/or (c) candidate constraints are submitted to constraint refinement,

(vi) constraint refinement, wherein for at least one candidate constraint, when a constraint testing according to specified strength metrics yields strength metrics values that fulfill certain predefined conditions indicating that the constraint is at least one from not sufficiently satisfied and/or is improvable, a constraint tightening is attempted by combining the at least one constraint with at least one from further restrictions,
and/or conditions,
 and/
or constraints, thereby producing one or more new constraints, of which those meeting predefined acceptability criteria are retained as candidate constraints,
(vii) rule set testing, wherein rules in the set of selected candidate constraints are compared with other rules in the same set in order to single out redundant rules that are subsumed or implied by other rules or sets of rules in the set of selected candidate constraints, and
(viii) final constraint selection, wherein redundant rules are eliminated and a final set of rules is output or made available for further processing.
Currently amended

13
13. (Original) The method of claim 12, 
wherein at least one sample database is generated from the database by automatically picking or sampling data tuples from the database, the automatically picking or sampling data tuples from the database comprising fixing an integer distance constant d, picking a plurality of starting tuples from the database, each starting tuple belonging to a relation of the database, and, for each picked starting tuple, adding related tuples

that are connected by a join-chain of length at most d to the picked starting tuple, wherein a join- chain is a chain in which two successive tuples t and t' join, that is, for at least one attribute A of the relation t belongs to, and for at least one attribute A' of the relation t' belongs to, the A-value of
 t coincides with the A'-value oft', formally, t[A]=t' [A'].
Original

14
14. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 13, 
wherein at least one from:
(i) the starting tuples are chosen among tuples having at least one value that occurs infrequently in the database, and/or (ii) starting tuples are randomly selected, and/or
 (iii) the related tuples are constructed via a set of join chains, wherein for join chains starting with one of the starting tuples and having length i<d, and whose last element has a value v for attribute A, a set S of new tuples is added, wherein at least one from:(a) S consists of all new tuples having v as an argument, and/or (b) S consists of one or more new tuples having v as an argument, and/or
 (c) S consists of at least one new tuple selected randomly among new tuples.

Currently amended

15
15. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 12, 
wherein the bag-template based rule-generation comprises:
(i) fixing a constant k expressing a maximum rule length,
(ii) selecting or more relation names among the names of relations of the database,

(iii) for each relation name of the one or more relation names, fixing a multiplicity, the multiplicity being a small constant expressing how often the relation name or a corresponding predicate name shall appear in a rule body, the sum of the multiplicities being at most k,
(iv) optionally selecting a rule-head predicate,
(v) issuing a prompt to the one or more LLMs, the prompt referring to at least one from the database and/or to results of previously performed database understanding, and asking the one or more LLMs to generate TGDs assumed to be true in the database, wherein each generated TGD:(a) has in its body at most k database atoms, wherein a database atom is an atom whose predicate is a database predicate, and
(b) when a rule-head predicate has been selected, has a head atom whose predicate is the selected head predicate, and
(c) has a rule body in which the selected database names occur with the specified multiplicity in the body of the TGD, such so
that if the selected multiplicity of a selected relation name R is r, then there are exactly r occurrences of atoms of the form R(...) in the rule body of the TGD, wherein atoms with database atoms maypredicates are allowed to be negated, and wherein no further atoms except comparison atoms may occur in the rule,

(vi) any TGD rules generated by the prompt and extracted from the prompt answer are recorded as rule candidates, and
(vii) the steps (ii)-(v) are repeated by varying at least one from the selected rule head predicate,
and/or the selected database names,
 and/or the multiplicities[[,]].

Currently amended

16
16. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 12, 
wherein the constraint testing is performed by generating one or more computer programs or database queries that evaluate the constraint over the the database and return constraint strength values according to the computed strength metrics.
Currently amended

17
17. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 16, 
wherein at least one from:
(a) the computed strength metrics are support and confidence, and/or (b) the one or more computer programs or database queries are generated by at least one prompt, the at least one prompt submitted to the one or more LLMs, the at least one prompt being automatically constructed and containing all necessary information relevant to the generation of the one or more computer programs or database queries, the one or more prompts further containing the request to generate one or more computer programs or database queries in the specified programming language or database query language that evaluate the constraint over the database and return the constraint strength values according to the computed strength metrics, and/or
 (c) the constraint testing is performed by generating a database query in SQL.
Currently amended

18. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 12, 
wherein the set of tasks further comprises an alternative constraint testing, wherein one or more constraints from a group of constraints comprising semantic attribute-domain restrictions are tested using at least one from external resources and/or web-services.

Currently amended

19. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 12
2

wherein two or more sample databases are used for the constraint generation and wherein candidate constraint sets are generated separately for each of the two or more sample databases, and wherein from the candidate constraint sets a single consolidated candidate set is obtained by at least one from:
(a) selecting candidate constraints that occur in many or most of the generated candidate constraint sets, and/or (b) picking the strongest constraints according to some constraint strength evaluation from all generated candidate constraint sets, and/or (c) uniting some or all of the candidate constraint sets.
Currently amended

20. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 12, 
further comprising, when the database is relational, helping the generating at least one database constraint by prior identification, via automatically issued prompts, of column clusters and of at least one from relationships and/
or dependencies between column clusters, wherein a column cluster is a set of closely related attributes of the database, wherein the automatically issued prompts comprise one or more column correlation discovery prompts to identify column clusters based on the relatedness of attributes, and one or more column cluster correlation discovery prompts that ask for at least one from relationships and/or dependencies between the identified column clusters, after which the identified column clusters and the at least one from relationships and/or dependencies between column clusters are used with one or more rule generation prompts for the generating at least one database constraint .

Currently amended

21. (Original) The method of claim 12, 
wherein the computed strength metrics comprise confidence and support with respect to the database.
Original

22
22. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 21, 
wherein for at least one candidate constraint Cand the candidate constraint selection comprises the following actions, where s1,
cl
 and c2 are predefined threshold constants:
(i) if the support of Cand is smaller than s1
, then Cand is rejected, otherwise,(a) if the confidence of Cand is at least c2, then Cand is retained,
(b) if the confidence of Cand is at least c
l
 and less than c2, then Cand is submitted to constraint refinement, and is Cand is rejected when it cannot be refined.

Currently amended

23
23. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 12, 
wherein one or more of the generated database constraints are expressed as rules each comprising a rule head and a rule body, and wherein at least one of the rules is refined by at least one from:
(i) adding an atom into the rule body, thereby restricting the applicability of the rule, and/or (ii) adding an atom into the rule body, thereby restricting the applicability of the rule, and replacing the rule head with a new rule head better suited for situations in which the restricted rule body applies, and/or (iii) determining a pivot atom and replacing the at least one rule by two new rules, one new rule by adding the pivot atom into the body of the at least one rule and the other new rule by adding a literal consisting of the negated pivot atom into the body of the at least one rule, and

replacing for at least one of the two new rules the rule head by a more appropriate rule head better suited for situations in which the corresponding restricted rule body applies, and/or (iv) replacing the at least one rule with at least two new rules according to at least two cases, wherein for each case, the at least one rule is replaced by a new rule by inserting into the rule body of the at least one rule one or more literals representing the case, and by adapting one or more rule heads of the new rules according to the corresponding cases, and wherein each of the one or more literals is an atom or negated atom selected from a group of atoms comprising:i. a database atom whose predicate symbol is or corresponds to a relation name from the database,
ii. a numerical or lexicographical comparison expression,
iii. an arithmetic formula expressing a condition or an assignment to a variable,
and iv. a call to an external procedure.

Currently amended

24
24. (Original) The method of claim 12, 
wherein one or more counterexamples to satisfaction of at least one of the at least one database constraints by the database are computed by one of:

(i) a direct counterexample generation via at least one counterexample prompt automatically composed and issued to the one or more LLMs, the at least one counterexample prompt comprising asking to find counterexamples to the satisfaction by the database of the at least one database constraint, or of one or more constraints of a specified constraint type and collecting counterexamples from the prompt answer or prompt answers, or

(ii) an indirect counterexample generation by at least one LLM-generated database query program, wherein the following steps are executed:
(a) automatically generating at least one computer program prompt, the at least one computer program prompt containing information about the database, the at least one constraint or at least one constraint type, the at least one computer program prompt further containing a request to compute a database query program in a specified programming language or in a specified query language, the database query program computing counterexamples to the satisfaction of the at least one constraint or for a constraint of the at least one constraint type by the database,
(b) submitting the at least one computer program prompt to the one or more LLMs and extracting the at least one database query program from the LLM answers, and
(c) the at least one database query program is issued against the database and outputs counterexamples to the satisfaction by the database of the at least one constraint or of one or more constraints of the specified constraint type.

Original

25
25. (Currently Amended) The method of claim 24, 
wherein at least one from:
(a) the at least one counterexample prompt further contains a request to exclude recognized data errors from the counterexamples, and/or (b) the at least one computer program prompt further contains a request to generate a database query program that excludes recognized data errors from the output, and/or (c) the at least one computer program prompt asks to exclude recognized data errors from the counterexamples, and/or

(d) the at least one counterexample prompt asks for counterexamples that are outliers, and/or (e) the at least one computer program prompt further contains the request that the counterexamples returned by the database query program be outliers, and/or (f) the specified query language is SQL, and/or (g) the specified programming language is Prolog, and/or (h) for at least one specified constraint type, one or more constraints and one or more counterexamples to the the satisfaction by the database of the one or more constraints of the specified constraint type are computed automatically by use of a single prompt to the one or more LLMs.

Currently amended

26
26. (Original) The method of claim 12, 
wherein the constraint refinement comprises, for at least one constraint to be refined, using counterexamples to the satisfaction of the at least one constraint to be refined by the database as part of a refinement prompt issued to the one or more LLMs, the refinement prompt asking for refinements of the at least one constraint to be refined, wherein a counterexample to the satisfaction of the at least one constraint to be refined by the database comprises a small sub-structure of the database that provides evidence that the at least one constraint to be refined is violated.
Original
